cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out when Thomas could have gone to the football field. The question is asking for the possible time window when Thomas was not busy with other activities, and also before the football field closed at 9pm. First, let me list out all the times Thomas was doing other things: 1. Thomas woke up at 8am. (So he was at home, not doing anything else yet?) 2. David saw Thomas fixing their computer at the electronic store from 8am to 10am. So from 8am to 10am, Thomas was at the electronic store. 3. Jennifer saw Thomas buying lunch at the deli from 10am to 12pm. So from 10am to 12pm, he was at the deli. 4. Lisa saw Thomas walking towards the Statue of Liberty from 12pm to 2pm. So from 12pm to 2pm, he was heading to or at the Statue of Liberty. 5. Sarah saw Thomas buying a phone at the electronics store from 2pm to 5pm. So from 2pm to 5pm, he was at the electronics store. 6. Kimberly saw Thomas buying a bike at the bike shop from 6pm to 9pm. So from 6pm to 9pm, he was at the bike shop. 7. The football field was closed after 9pm. So Thomas had to be at the football field before 9pm. Now, let's see when Thomas was free. Let me create a timeline of his activities: - 8am to 10am: Electronic store (fixing computer) - 10am to 12pm: Deli (buying lunch) - 12pm to 2pm: Walking to Statue of Liberty - 2pm to 5pm: Electronic store (buying phone) - 6pm to 9pm: Bike shop (buying bike) So between these activities, when was Thomas free? - From 8am to 10am: occupied - From 10am to 12pm: occupied - From 12pm to 2pm: occupied - From 2pm to 5pm: occupied - From 5pm to 6pm: free? Because after 2pm to 5pm he was at the electronic store, then from 5pm to 6pm is a gap, and then from 6pm to 9pm he was at the bike shop. So Thomas was free from 5pm to 6pm. During that time, he could have gone to the football field, and the football field was still open (closed after 9pm).
